libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use Conscrypt directly via `Conscrypt.newProvider()` or explicit provider selection; no external server is required because meaningful TLS/SSLEngine coverage can use in-memory handshakes or loopback sockets. Preserve/add the existing test and native-image build arguments `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`, `--add-opens=java.base/java.net=ALL-UNNAMED`, and `--add-opens=java.base/sun.nio.ch=ALL-UNNAMED` when covering socket wrapping, channel-backed sockets, or file-descriptor paths. Do not set `org.conscrypt.native.workdir` unless local execution reports that the temp directory is mounted `noexec`.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Conscrypt is JNI-backed and extracts `META-INF/native/libconscrypt_openjdk_jni-*` from the uber JAR; native-image failures are more likely metadata/resource/JNI issues than missing dependencies.
JDK 25 emits restricted native-access warnings for `System.load`; local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
If tests use only high-level provider APIs they may pass without the module opens, but socket/channel coverage can fail under strong encapsulation if the opens are removed.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed; the uber artifact has no declared dependencies and embeds its native libraries, but meaningful socket/channel coverage needs JVM/native-image module opens already present in the existing scaffold.
